title: Compania electorală post-realitate de la Budapesta
summary: Anne Applebaum analyzes Viktor Orbán's unprecedented cognitive warfare campaign ahead of Hungarian elections, involving AI-generated deepfakes, Russian propaganda support, and international far-right solidarity. Despite 16 years of governance that made Hungary the EU's most corrupt and least free member, Fidesz diverts attention from domestic failures by fabricating an existential Ukrainian threat to mobilize voters.
talking_points: Orbán's party uses AI-generated deepfakes on TikTok depicting Zelensky and opposition leader Péter Magyar in fabricated scenarios to manipulate voters
Russia's Kremlin-backed Social Design Agency has deployed propagandists to Budapest, creating bot networks and even proposing staging an assassination attempt on Orbán as a 'Gamechanger' strategy
The Trump administration actively supports Orbán, with Rubio visiting Budapest, Vance planning a visit, and Trump sending a video message offering 'full and total support'
After 16 years in power, Fidesz has made Hungary the EU's most corrupt, poorest, and least free country, yet campaigns entirely on fabricated external threats rather than domestic issues
International far-right leaders from Germany, Spain, France, Poland, Netherlands, Israel, and Argentina rallied at CPAC Budapest in a show of authoritarian solidarity
author: Anne Applebaum
